frame_analyses.description: Instructor holds green 2-0 silk suture and displays Roeder's knot technique reference material (black and white instructional photos showing knot tying steps A through F)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational framing; demonstrating knot tying methodology with visual reference

frame_analyses.description: Hands visible in bare setup, green and black suture ends visible on blue pad with white foam block workpiece
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Preparing workspace; suture positioned for demonstration

frame_analyses.description: Right hand (dominant) grasps green suture end, left hand (non-dominant) positioned to begin first throw. Hands in position with scissors visible in background
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean hand positioning; appropriate tension on suture

frame_analyses.description: First throw being formed; right hand wrapping suture around left hand's index and middle fingers. Proper looping motion visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Textbook Roeder's knot formation; smooth, controlled wrap

frame_analyses.description: First throw cinching; right hand pulling suture end while left hand stabilizes the loop. Knot tightening clearly visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Even tension application; knot appears secure and symmetrical

frame_analyses.description: Second throw initiated; hands repositioned. Left hand now leading the throw. Text overlay reads 'ADJUST TO GAIN TIGHT FRICTION'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching demonstrated; appropriate friction control being verbally cued

frame_analyses.description: Second throw formation in progress. Left hand wraps suture while right hand maintains tension on tail
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean alternating hand technique; consistent with expert methodology

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being tightened. Knot visible at foam block position; tension being applied uniformly
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth throw completion; knot alignment appears centered on workpiece

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being initiated. Left hand positioning for next wrap. Hands show practiced rhythm
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Efficient transition between throws; no hesitation or instrument repositioning

frame_analyses.description: Third throw cinching. Right hand pulling suture while left stabilizes. Knot tightening visible on foam block
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final throw executed with appropriate tension; knot appears fully cinched

frame_analyses.description: Knot fully tied and seated on foam block. Suture tails extending from knot. No visible gap in foam block slit
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Complete knot formation; slit appears fully closed by knot tension

frame_analyses.description: Scissors visible in hand approaching suture tails. Preparation to cut both suture ends inside trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final step ready to execute; proper instrument positioning

penalties.description: No suture deviations from marks, no gap in slit closure, no knot slippage, no drain avulsion observed

knot_assessments.notes: Clean formation and tightening; appropriate knot geometry and tension
Demonstrating proper hand alternation; smooth wrap and cinch
Final throw cinched with good tension; knot fully secured

suture_placement.notes: Workpiece is foam block; suture passes through marked targets with precision

drain_assessment.notes: Foam block slit fully closed by final knot tension; no residual gapping observed

confidence_rationale: This is a clear expert demonstration video with excellent visibility of all phases. Knot formation, hand switching, and knot security are all visible and perfect. Suture placement and slit closure are unambiguous. The only minor limitation is that the video shows instructional framing (reference photos) at the start, but the hands-on execution is crisp and observable throughout.

demonstrated_techniques: Roeder's knot formation with alternating hand throws
Proper friction control during knot cinching ('ADJUST TO GAIN TIGHT FRICTION')
Hand-to-hand transfer and wrap technique for extracorporeal knot
Sequential single throws with consistent tension
Knot positioning on workpiece to close slit fully
verbal_cues: ADJUST TO GAIN TIGHT FRICTION (frame ~6, 25s mark)
common_errors_addressed: Proper hand alternation to maintain knot strength and symmetry
Avoiding loose or asymmetrical knots by controlling wrap and cinch tension
Ensuring full slit closure by applying appropriate final knot tension

technique_summary: Expert-level demonstration of extracorporeal suture with three single throws using Roeder's knot technique. All phases executed flawlessly with smooth hand transitions, perfect knot security, and complete slit closure. Completion time of 55 seconds is significantly faster than the proficiency benchmark of 136 seconds, reflecting expert-level efficiency and muscle memory.
strengths: Textbook hand alternation between throws with no hesitation
Consistent, appropriate tension throughout knot formation and cinching
Perfect knot geometry and alignment on workpiece
Complete slit closure with no gapping
Efficient workflow with minimal extraneous motion
improvement_suggestions: None applicable; this is expert-level performance suitable for instruction and reference
